rent.note: furnished 1BR in central nomad-friendly neighborhood; range $450–$800

utilities.note: electric + water + fiber internet; mild highland climate keeps bills low

groceries.note: home cooking; large mercados + Walmart/Costco present

diningOut.note: diverse food scene; cemitas and mole affordable

transit.note: buses/combis + Uber; direct bus to CDMX 2hr

coworking.note: hot-desk monthly; growing coworking and university city culture

accomSeasonality._doc: 12 monthly multipliers for RENT ONLY (Jan..Dec). 1.0 = base.

evidence.quote: Puebla is among the lowest-cost nomad cities with consistently workable Wi-Fi, realistic monthly budgets of $1,000–$1,500 USD. A 'cheap-but-comfortable' solo nomad budget of $1,200/month includes rent of $450–$750

evidence.claim: living costs 30–35% below CDMX; frugal at ~$900/month
evidence.quote: Living costs in Puebla are 30%–35% lower than in the capital. For those seeking the lowest costs, Puebla comes in at roughly $900/month for a single person

evidence.claim: 1BR 8,000–14,000 pesos/month (~$460–$800 USD) in safe central neighborhoods
evidence.quote: In Puebla, one-bedroom apartments often fall into the 8,000 to 14,000 peso monthly range in reasonably central, safe neighborhoods

evidence.claim: Puebla digital nomad overview on nomads.com
evidence.quote: Puebla is a great city for digital nomads looking for affordable living with strong urban infrastructure
